claim: The text-only section (7 folios, entropy 3.9016 bits â€” highest of all sections) represents unenciphered or minimally-enciphered natural language, while all other sections apply an additional cipher layer, making text-only the most direct window into the underlying language and the optimal starting point for statistical language identification.
evidence_for: Text-only section entropy (3.9016) is closest to natural Latin (~4.0) and Italian (~3.95) among all sections
Absence of illustrations in text-only sections may indicate a different compositional mode
If some sections are more heavily enciphered, the least-enciphered section should show highest entropy (closer to plaintext)
evidence_against: 7 folios and 2,349 words is a small sample â€” entropy estimate has high variance
Higher entropy could reflect larger domain vocabulary rather than reduced encipherment
Current beliefs favor herbal section (0.245) as best starting point, not text-only
test: Compute the Kullback-Leibler divergence D_KL(text-only glyph distribution || section_X glyph distribution) for all other sections X; also compare text-only unigram and bigram distributions against frequency-matched medieval Latin and Italian corpora using chi-squared goodness-of-fit
test_metric: KL divergence between text-only and each other section; chi-squared p-value for fit to Latin vs. Italian character frequency profiles
test_threshold: KL divergence to natural-language profile below 0.05 bits for text-only but above 0.15 for herbal/biological would confirm differential encipherment; chi-squared p above 0.10 for one target language would identify candidate plaintext language

tests_run.test: compute the kullback-leibler divergence d_kl(text-only glyph distribution || section_x glyph distrib
tests_run.details: Section entropy std dev: 0.0593. Range: 3.7149 - 3.9016. Variable across sections.

reasoning: Identifying whether one section is less enciphered than others would allow targeted cryptanalysis on that section first, potentially yielding a key applicable to other sections â€” this directly addresses the decipherment strategy question encoded in belief 0.245.
